title: Analyze & record first sources (CIViC/Reactome/Open Targets; flag COSMIC/OncoKB/DrugBank)
context: With the allow-list schema defined (oncogene-kg-license-001), the first concrete sources must be analyzed and their license determinations recorded. CIViC is expected to be approved (CC0). Reactome and Open Targets each require careful analysis: Open Targets distinguishes between its own CC0 computed data and the integrated third-party sources it aggregates. COSMIC, OncoKB, and DrugBank-NC must be formally recorded as rejected with specific reasons.
objective: Analyze ≥3 candidate sources (CIViC, Reactome, Open Targets) and record each as an entry in sources/allowlist.yml with a license/redistribution determination. Formally record COSMIC, OncoKB, and DrugBank-NC as rejected with specific reasoning. Note the Open Targets distinction between OT-own CC0 data and integrated upstream source data.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 sources analyzed with a recorded license/redistribution determination in sources/allowlist.yml.
CIViC is marked approved (CC0-1.0) with evidence (license URL, retrieved date, version).
Reactome is analyzed with redistribution and attribution obligations recorded.
Open Targets is analyzed with the OT-own-CC0 vs integrated-source-license distinction explicitly noted.
COSMIC, OncoKB, and DrugBank-NC are recorded as rejected with the specific reason (non-commercial/proprietary/NC clause).
Each entry includes the source version and URL at time of analysis.
output: Populated sources/allowlist.yml with entries for CIViC (approved), Reactome, Open Targets, COSMIC (rejected), OncoKB (rejected), and DrugBank-NC (rejected), plus a brief analysis memo in docs/source-license-analysis.md.
